description_long: This object is a cluster of small, vibrant purple-pink flowers arranged on branching, reddish-brown stems. Each flower has a tubular base with five rounded petals, forming a shallow cup shape, and a central cluster of yellowish stamens. The flowers are densely grouped, creating a lush, ornamental appearance typical of urban landscaping or decorative planters. The stems are smooth and slightly glossy, with no visible leaves attached. The overall structure is compact, with the flowers radiating outward from the central stem. The coloration and form suggest a species often used for visual appeal in parks, gardens, or sidewalk planters.
description: A dense cluster of purple-pink, cup-shaped flowers with yellow centers on reddish-brown stems, arranged in a compact, ornamental form.
description_view_0: The flowers are seen arching to the right, with the branching stems and clustered blooms clearly visible.
description_view_1: A more frontal view, showing the dense grouping of flowers and the central stem, with blooms facing forward.
description_view_2: The flowers are angled to the left, revealing the layered arrangement and the depth of the cluster.
description_view_3: A rear view, highlighting the branching structure and the back sides of the flowers, with the stems more prominent.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a radial configuration, with each flower displaying five overlapping, rounded petals forming a shallow cup. The arrangement is symmetrical and whorled, typical of many ornamental flowering plants.
